notes: Three claudecode lifetimes back-to-back, one per session-end variant in .specs/agent-scenarios.md. Variant 1 ends via Ctrl-D clean exit; variants 2 and 3 end via SIGKILL (one mid-idle, one mid-turn). The grey regions between variants in the viewer's state band are intentional — they show times when no session is alive (between one process_exited and the next pre-session_created).
variant 1: pre-session row appears in ready ≤1s after claude launch
variant 1: UUID transcript materializes on first user input
variant 1: Expected "State is ready at exit" — Ctrl-D clean shutdown after the turn
variant 2: pre-session for the second lifetime
variant 2: turn complete; session goes idle in ready
variant 2: Expected "Session disappears within 1s; state never entered working post-exit" — SIGKILL fires while idle, process_exited follows ~1.3s later
variant 3: pre-session for the third lifetime
variant 3: assistant begins streaming the essay

variant 3: Expected "State reaches ready within sweep interval; does not stick in working" — SIGKILL fires mid-stream; the recording captures the working→exit shape. Sweep-to-ready may not appear in the 8s tail; rely on the live dashboard's sweep-after-process-exit to resolve.
